name: Gila monster saliva → exenatide (GLP-1 agonist) for T2D
description: Exendin-4 peptide in Gila monster saliva resists DPP-4 degradation and activates the GLP-1 receptor in mammals
outcome: Exenatide (Byetta, 2005); established GLP-1R agonist class (semaglutide, liraglutide, etc.)
refs: Eng et al. 1992 JBC doi:10.1016/S0021-9258(18)41961-1; DeFronzo et al. 2005 Diabetes Care doi:10.2337/diacare.28.5.1092

name: Cone snail venom → ziconotide (Cav2.2 blocker) for chronic pain
description: ω-conotoxin MVIIA selectively blocks N-type voltage-gated calcium channels (Cav2.2) in spinal dorsal horn
outcome: Ziconotide (Prialt, FDA 2004) for severe refractory chronic pain, intrathecal
refs: Olivera et al. 1985 Science doi:10.1126/science.2578759; Staats et al. 2004 JAMA doi:10.1001/jama.291.1.63

name: Pit viper venom → captopril (ACE inhibitor) for hypertension
description: Bradykinin-potentiating peptides in venom inhibit angiotensin-converting enzyme
outcome: Captopril (1981); founded the ACE inhibitor class, one of the most successful antihypertensive classes
refs: Ferreira 1965 Br J Pharmacol; Cushman & Ondetti 1999 Hypertension doi:10.1161/01.HYP.34.4.863

name: Leech saliva → hirudin/bivalirudin (thrombin inhibitors)
description: Hirudin is a direct thrombin inhibitor secreted by medicinal leeches; binds thrombin active site and exosite I with extreme specificity
outcome: Bivalirudin (Angiomax, 2000); lepirudin; desirudin — direct thrombin inhibitor class
refs: Markwardt 1970 Methods Enzymol; Bittl et al. 1995 NEJM doi:10.1056/NEJM199502093320602

name: Camelid heavy-chain antibodies → caplacizumab for aTTP
description: Camelids produce heavy-chain-only antibodies (HCAbs); their VHH domain (nanobody, ~15 kDa) is monomeric, stable, and accesses epitopes IgG cannot
outcome: Caplacizumab (Cablivi, FDA 2019) anti-vWF nanobody for acquired thrombotic thrombocytopenic purpura
refs: Hamers-Casterman et al. 1993 Nature doi:10.1038/363446a0; Scully et al. 2019 NEJM doi:10.1056/NEJMoa1806311

name: Horseshoe crab amebocyte lysate → LAL endotoxin test
description: Amebocytes coagulate in response to Gram-negative bacterial endotoxin via a Factor C serine protease cascade
outcome: LAL assay used worldwide for endotoxin testing of all parenteral drugs and medical devices since 1977
refs: Levin & Bang 1968 Thromb Diath Haemorrh; Iwanaga 2007 J Thromb Haemost doi:10.1111/j.1538-7836.2007.02500.x

name: Scorpion chlorotoxin → tumor paint / BLZ-100 (tozuleristide)
description: Chlorotoxin binds preferentially to glioma-associated MMP-2 and annexin A2 complexes on tumor cells
outcome: Tozuleristide (BLZ-100) fluorescent tumor imaging agent; Phase 2/3 trials for intraoperative tumor visualization
refs: DeBin et al. 1993 Am J Physiol; Patil et al. 2019 Neurosurgery doi:10.1093/neuros/nyy239

name: Cyclosporine from fungus Tolypocladium (immunosuppression)
description: Cyclic undecapeptide binds cyclophilin; inhibits calcineurin and blocks T-cell activation
outcome: Cyclosporine (Sandimmune, 1983); transformed organ transplantation and founded calcineurin inhibitor class
refs: Borel et al. 1976 Agents Actions doi:10.1007/BF01973261

name: Tunicate ET-743 → trabectedin (Yondelis) for sarcoma
description: Ecteinascidin-743 is an alkaloid from Caribbean tunicate that binds the minor groove of DNA, disrupting transcription and selectively killing cycling cells
outcome: Trabectedin (Yondelis, EMA 2007, FDA 2015) for soft tissue sarcoma and ovarian cancer
refs: Rinehart et al. 1990 J Org Chem; Demetri et al. 2016 J Clin Oncol doi:10.1200/JCO.2015.62.4734

name: Sponge Cryptotethya crypta → vidarabine/cytarabine (nucleoside analogs)
description: Caribbean sponge produces spongothymidine/spongouridine — unusual arabinose-containing nucleosides that became the template for antiviral and antineoplastic nucleoside analogs
outcome: Cytarabine (ara-C) approved 1969 for AML; vidarabine (ara-A) approved for HSV; founded the nucleoside-analog therapeutic class that underlies modern antivirals and leukemia therapy
refs: Bergmann & Feeney 1951 J Org Chem; Grant et al. 2007 Curr Opin Pharmacol

name: Sea squirt Aplidium → plitidepsin (aplidine) for myeloma
description: Cyclic depsipeptide from Mediterranean tunicate; binds eEF1A2 and induces apoptosis in tumor cells
outcome: Plitidepsin (Aplidin) approved in Australia 2018 for multiple myeloma; explored for COVID-19 in Phase 2/3
refs: Rinehart 2000 Med Res Rev; Losada et al. 2021 ChemMedChem doi:10.1002/cmdc.202100180

name: Cone snail conantokin → NMDAR research tools and epilepsy leads
description: Conantokin-G selectively antagonizes NR2B-containing NMDA receptors via γ-carboxyglutamate-dependent binding
outcome: Conantokin-G (CGX-1007) reached Phase 1 for intractable epilepsy; widely used as research probe for NR2B-selective NMDAR biology informing multiple programs
refs: Layer et al. 2004 J Pharmacol Exp Ther; Barton et al. 2004 Methods Mol Med

name: Vampire bat saliva DSPA → rediscovery as research tool
description: Plasminogen activator with fibrin specificity higher than human tPA; no NMDAR-mediated neurotoxicity in vitro
outcome: Reached Phase 3 (DIAS-3, DIAS-4); failed primary efficacy endpoint in extended-window ischemic stroke
refs: Reddrop et al. 2005 Stroke; Albers et al. 2015 Lancet Neurol doi:10.1016/S1474-4422(15)00199-3

name: Bacterial protein C activator → drotrecogin alfa (Xigris) for sepsis
species: Agkistrodon contortrix (copperhead) — Protac enzyme is viper-derived
description: Protac, a protein C activator from southern copperhead venom, activates human protein C; recombinant activated protein C was developed as anti-inflammatory anticoagulant
outcome: Drotrecogin alfa (Xigris) FDA-approved 2001 for severe sepsis; withdrawn 2011 after PROWESS-SHOCK failed to confirm benefit
refs: Stocker et al. 1987 Toxicon; Ranieri et al. 2012 NEJM doi:10.1056/NEJMoa1202290

name: Pig intestine heparin → unfractionated and LMW heparins
description: Heparin was discovered in canine liver and commercialized from porcine intestinal mucosa; sulfated glycosaminoglycan potentiates antithrombin III to inhibit thrombin and factor Xa
outcome: Unfractionated heparin (1930s), enoxaparin (1993), dalteparin, tinzaparin, fondaparinux — cornerstone of anticoagulation
refs: McLean 1916 Am J Physiol; Weitz 1997 NEJM doi:10.1056/NEJM199709043371007

name: Horse antibodies → antivenoms and early antitoxins (diphtheria, botulinum)
description: Horses generate high-titer polyclonal antibodies to toxins; F(ab')2 or Fab fragments neutralize venoms and bacterial toxins
outcome: Snake antivenoms (CroFab, etc.), diphtheria antitoxin, botulinum antitoxin (BAT) — sustained clinical use for over a century; replaced in some indications by human antibodies but still essential for envenomation
refs: Behring & Kitasato 1890 Dtsch Med Wochenschr; Lang et al. 1999 J Clin Pharmacol

name: Marine bryozoan Bugula neritina → bryostatin-1 for Alzheimer's/cancer
description: Bryostatin-1 is a macrolactone PKC modulator produced by bacterial symbiont of marine bryozoan; activates PKC-ε
outcome: Decades of clinical trials in cancer (numerous Phase 2, no approval) and more recently Alzheimer's disease (Phase 2 ambiguous results)
refs: Pettit et al. 1982 JACS; Farlow et al. 2019 J Prev Alzheimers Dis doi:10.14283/jpad.2019.13

name: Sea anemone ShK toxin → dalazatide (Kv1.3 blocker) for autoimmune
description: ShK peptide selectively blocks Kv1.3 voltage-gated potassium channel on effector memory T cells; engineered ShK-186 (dalazatide) preserves selectivity
outcome: Dalazatide reached Phase 1 for psoriasis with positive biomarker results; subsequent development stalled
refs: Castaneda et al. 1995 Toxicon; Tarcha et al. 2017 PLOS ONE doi:10.1371/journal.pone.0180762

name: Streptomyces bacteria → streptomycin, tetracyclines, macrolides
description: Soil actinomycetes produce polyketide and aminoglycoside antibiotics targeting bacterial ribosomes
outcome: Streptomycin (1944), tetracycline, erythromycin, neomycin — foundational antibiotic classes
refs: Schatz et al. 1944 Proc Soc Exp Biol Med; Waksman 1952 Nobel Lecture

name: Penicillium mold → penicillin and β-lactam class
description: Fungal secondary metabolite acylating penicillin-binding proteins; disrupts bacterial cell wall synthesis
outcome: Penicillin (1942), founded β-lactam class including cephalosporins, carbapenems
refs: Fleming 1929 Br J Exp Pathol; Chain et al. 1940 Lancet

name: Pacific yew tree → paclitaxel (Taxol)
description: Diterpenoid from yew bark stabilizes microtubules, blocking mitosis
outcome: Paclitaxel (Taxol, 1992) and docetaxel — cornerstone oncology drugs for breast, ovarian, lung cancer
refs: Wani et al. 1971 JACS; Schiff et al. 1979 Nature doi:10.1038/277665a0

name: Sea cucumber cytotoxins → frondoside A and related preclinical leads
description: Triterpene glycosides from sea cucumbers show broad antitumor activity via membrane disruption and apoptosis induction
outcome: Frondoside A — preclinical only; sulfated polysaccharide (fucoidan) preparations sold as supplements but no approved therapeutic
refs: Aminin et al. 2015 Mar Drugs doi:10.3390/md13031202

name: Magnolia officinalis → honokiol/magnolol research leads
description: Honokiol is a biphenolic compound with GABA-A modulating and anti-inflammatory activity; traditional Chinese medicine with modern mechanism studies
outcome: Multiple Phase 1/2 cancer and anxiety trials; no approved pharmaceutical despite decades of interest
refs: Maruyama et al. 1998 Gen Pharmacol; Rauf et al. 2021 Biomed Pharmacother

name: Fire ant venom solenopsin → pharmacological tool with limited translation
description: Solenopsin alkaloids in fire ant venom have antiangiogenic and anti-inflammatory activity; inhibit PI3K-Akt pathway
outcome: Preclinical investigations only; topical preparations for psoriasis in animal models; no clinical development
refs: Arbiser et al. 2007 Blood doi:10.1182/blood-2007-02-075648

name: Tarantula psalmotoxin → ASIC1a research tool
description: PcTx1 selectively inhibits homomeric ASIC1a; used extensively as pharmacological tool in pain and ischemia research
outcome: Research tool only; inspired ASIC-targeting programs but no approved therapeutic
refs: Escoubas et al. 2000 J Biol Chem; Duan et al. 2011 J Neurosci

name: Black mamba venom → mambalgin peptides (ASIC blockers)
description: Mambalgin peptides selectively block ASIC1a/ASIC2a; potent non-opioid analgesia in mouse pain models
outcome: Preclinical; multiple analogs in development; validated in human DRG neurons but no clinical trials as of 2024
refs: Diochot et al. 2012 Nature doi:10.1038/nature11494

name: Cone snail Conus geographus → ultra-rapid insulin analog design
description: Weaponized insulin in predatory cone snails is structurally monomeric and fast-acting; informs ultra-rapid human insulin design
outcome: Structural insights informing ultra-rapid insulin analogs (preclinical); mini-insulin designs in development
refs: Safavi-Hemami et al. 2015 PNAS doi:10.1073/pnas.1423857112

name: Tree frog Phyllomedusa → dermorphin-inspired opioids and pexiganan
description: Skin secretions contain opioid peptides and antimicrobials; dermorphin is a μ-opioid agonist with high potency
outcome: Pexiganan (magainin derivative) reached Phase 3 for diabetic foot infection — failed; dermorphin remained research tool; no approvals from this source
refs: Erspamer 1989 PNAS; Lipsky et al. 2008 Clin Infect Dis

name: Naked mole-rat HMM-HA → anti-cancer/longevity therapeutic
description: HMM-HA produced by HAS2 variant engages CD44 to trigger early contact inhibition via p16INK4a; confers cancer resistance in NMR
outcome: No human therapeutic has emerged despite a decade of work; HA-based drugs (viscosupplements) exist but do not replicate the NMR mechanism
refs: Tian et al. 2013 Nature doi:10.1038/nature12234; Takasugi et al. 2020 Nat Commun doi:10.1038/s41467-020-15882-w

name: Elephant TP53 retrogenes → direct clinical translation
description: ~20 TP53 retrogene copies in elephant genome confer DNA-damage hypersensitive apoptosis
outcome: No faithful translation path — humans have one TP53; MDM2 inhibitors (same pathway concept) have mixed record
refs: Abegglen et al. 2015 JAMA doi:10.1001/jama.2015.13134; Konopleva et al. 2020 Leukemia

name: Bat NLRP3 dampening → MCC950 class inflammasome inhibitors
description: Bats show dampened NLRP3 inflammasome via splice variant, reduced priming, and LRR modification
outcome: MCC950 (CRID3) hepatotoxicity halted development; successors in trials; no approvals
refs: Ahn et al. 2019 Nat Microbiol; Mangan et al. 2018 Nat Rev Drug Discov

name: Shark squalamine → antiangiogenic for AMD/cancer
description: Cationic aminosterol from dogfish shark with broad antiangiogenic and antimicrobial activity
outcome: Phase 3 AMD trials failed primary endpoint; cancer development discontinued
refs: Moore et al. 1993 PNAS doi:10.1073/pnas.90.4.1354

name: Acomys scar-free regeneration → human wound healing therapies
description: Spiny mice regenerate skin, hair follicles, cartilage without scarring; blastema-like structures; altered ECM composition
outcome: No specific Acomys-derived therapeutic; exploratory only
refs: Seifert et al. 2012 Nature doi:10.1038/nature11499

name: Bowhead whale CIRBP → longevity/DNA-repair therapeutic
description: Elevated CIRBP expression drives enhanced DSB repair
outcome: No clinical development; preclinical only as of 2025
refs: Firsanov et al. 2025 Nature doi:10.1038/s41586-025-09694-5

name: Hibernation torpor → human synthetic torpor / space medicine
description: Controlled hypometabolic state with preserved organ function; 90% CBF reduction without damage in ground squirrels
outcome: A1 adenosine agonists, 5'-AMP, H2S programs — no clinical success
refs: Frerichs et al. 1994 JCBFM; Hrvatin et al. 2020 Nature

name: Spalax concerted cell death / IFN-β → cancer therapy
description: Blind mole-rat fibroblasts release IFN-β on hyperproliferation, triggering concerted necrotic death
outcome: Interferon therapy broadly used in oncology but not specifically Spalax-inspired
refs: Gorbunova et al. 2012 PNAS doi:10.1073/pnas.1217211109

name: Tasmanian devil DFTD → cancer vaccine insights
description: Transmissible tumor with MHC downregulation; immune evasion insights
outcome: Academic interest only; no therapeutic program
refs: Murchison et al. 2010 Cell; Siddle et al. 2013 PNAS

name: Woolly mammoth cold-adapted hemoglobin → blood substitutes
description: Mammoth hemoglobin shows reduced oxygen affinity at low temperature
outcome: Structural insights only; no clinical product
refs: Campbell et al. 2010 Nat Genet doi:10.1038/ng.574

name: NMR ribosome fidelity → proteostasis drugs
description: NMR ribosomes have a 28S rRNA cleavage proposed to confer improved translation fidelity
outcome: Academic only; no drug program
refs: Azpurua et al. 2013 PNAS doi:10.1073/pnas.1217508110

name: Deer antler regeneration → human limb/bone regrowth
description: Antlers regrow ~2 cm/day; controlled proliferation without neoplasia
outcome: No clinical translation; supplement-grade products only
refs: Wang et al. 2019 Science doi:10.1126/science.aav6335

name: Bat longevity (GHR/IGF1R variants) → lifespan therapy
description: Mutations in GHR and IGF1R signaling proposed to underlie outlier longevity
outcome: GHR antagonists exist for acromegaly; no lifespan indication
refs: Seim et al. 2013 Nat Commun doi:10.1038/ncomms3212

name: Polar bear APOB lipid handling → atherosclerosis therapy
description: Polar bears subsist on >30% fat diet without atherosclerosis; APOB sequence variants identified
outcome: PCSK9/APOB drugs exist but not polar-bear-derived; specific variants not translated
refs: Liu et al. 2014 Cell doi:10.1016/j.cell.2014.03.054

name: Giraffe cardiovascular resistance → hypertension/heart failure
description: Giraffes maintain 280/180 mmHg blood pressure without left ventricular hypertrophy or kidney damage; FGFRL1 and fibrosis pathway variants identified
outcome: Genomic interest only; no drug program has emerged targeting giraffe-specific variants
refs: Agaba et al. 2016 Nat Commun doi:10.1038/ncomms11519

name: Weddell seal myoglobin engineering → blood substitute
description: High-charge myoglobin surface prevents aggregation at extreme concentrations; structural basis for diving hypoxia tolerance
outcome: Structural insights; no clinical myoglobin engineering program has delivered a product
refs: Mirceta et al. 2013 Science doi:10.1126/science.1234192

name: Cephalopod RNA editing → human transcriptome engineering
description: Cephalopods perform extensive ADAR-mediated A-to-I RNA editing to diversify neural proteins; unique among metazoans
outcome: Academic interest in ADAR for programmable RNA editing; multiple preclinical programs but no approved therapeutic
refs: Liscovitch-Brauer et al. 2017 Cell doi:10.1016/j.cell.2017.03.025

name: Planarian regeneration → human regenerative medicine
description: Planarians regenerate entire bodies from tissue fragments via pluripotent neoblasts; piwi-family genes maintain pluripotency
outcome: Extensive academic interest; insights into stem cell biology but no direct therapeutic
refs: Reddien 2018 Cell doi:10.1016/j.cell.2018.09.021

name: Axolotl limb regeneration → human regenerative medicine
description: Axolotls regenerate limbs, spinal cord, and heart tissue; blastema formation driven by dedifferentiation and Wnt/BMP signaling
outcome: Decades of research; no approved regenerative therapy from axolotl biology
refs: Kragl et al. 2009 Nature; Nowoshilow et al. 2018 Nature doi:10.1038/nature25458

name: Zebrafish heart regeneration → human cardiac repair
description: Zebrafish regenerate >20% ventricular resection via cardiomyocyte dedifferentiation and proliferation; Nrg1/ErbB2 signaling central
outcome: Extensive preclinical work; one ErbB2-directed program (Paragon) advanced but no approved cardiac regeneration therapy
refs: Poss et al. 2002 Science; D'Uva et al. 2015 Nat Cell Biol doi:10.1038/ncb3149

name: Electric eel electrocytes → bioelectric medicine
description: Electrocytes generate 600V discharges via stacked Na+/K+-ATPase and nicotinic receptors; informs bioelectronic medicine concepts
outcome: Inspiration for bioelectronic medicine but no direct therapeutic from eel biology
refs: Gallant et al. 2014 Science doi:10.1126/science.1254432

name: Tardigrade Dsup protein → human radioprotection
description: Damage suppressor protein (Dsup) binds chromatin and protects DNA from hydroxyl radical damage; expressed in human cells confers radioresistance
outcome: Preclinical validation in human cell lines; no clinical development
refs: Hashimoto et al. 2016 Nat Commun doi:10.1038/ncomms12808

name: Giant panda bamboo digestion → human metabolic insights
description: Despite short carnivore gut, panda digests bamboo via microbiome and host genome adaptations in taste and amylase
outcome: No therapeutic program; evolutionary curiosity
refs: Xue et al. 2015 mBio doi:10.1128/mBio.00022-15

name: Wood frog freeze tolerance → organ preservation
description: Wood frogs survive freezing via urea/glucose cryoprotection, AFP-like mechanisms, and controlled ice nucleation
outcome: Organ preservation research influenced by mechanism but no specific frog-derived clinical product
refs: Storey & Storey 2017 Physiol Rev doi:10.1152/physrev.00016.2016

name: Pufferfish tetrodotoxin → analgesic development
description: Tetrodotoxin selectively blocks voltage-gated sodium channels; present in skin/liver of pufferfish
outcome: Tectin (tetrodotoxin injection) reached Phase 3 for cancer-related pain; mixed results, not approved in US; approved in some countries for palliative use
refs: Narahashi 2008 Proc Jpn Acad Ser B; Hagen et al. 2017 J Pain Symptom Manage

name: Bombina frog bombesin → diagnostic/therapeutic peptides
description: Bombesin peptide from frog skin is a GRP receptor agonist with pharmacological activity on CNS, GI, and tumor cells
outcome: Extensive research; no approved bombesin-based therapeutic; radiolabeled analogs in clinical imaging development
refs: Anastasi et al. 1971 Experientia; Moody et al. 2018 Front Endocrinol

name: Jellyfish GFP → therapeutic (vs. research tool)
description: GFP fluorescence used as research reagent; not itself a therapeutic
outcome: Transformative research tool; no therapeutic product
refs: Shimomura 1962 J Cell Comp Physiol; Chalfie et al. 1994 Science

name: Hydra immortality / stem cells → aging therapy
description: Hydra shows negligible senescence; FoxO-dependent stem cell maintenance
outcome: FoxO pathway interest exists but no hydra-derived therapeutic has emerged
refs: Boehm et al. 2012 PNAS doi:10.1073/pnas.1209714109

name: Salamander heart regeneration (similar to zebrafish)
description: Newt cardiomyocytes dedifferentiate and re-enter cell cycle after injury; similar to zebrafish mechanism
outcome: No translation; mechanisms inform but do not produce therapeutics
refs: Laube et al. 2006 J Cell Sci; Godwin et al. 2017 Curr Top Dev Biol

name: Elephant LIF6 resurrection → apoptosis therapy
description: LIF6 is a resurrected pseudogene expressed in elephants on p53 activation; localizes to mitochondria to induce apoptosis
outcome: No human counterpart exists; no clinical translation
refs: Vazquez et al. 2018 Cell Reports doi:10.1016/j.celrep.2018.07.042

name: Bat constitutive IFN-α → viral tolerance therapy
description: Contracted type-I IFN locus with constitutive IFN-α expression in healthy tissue
outcome: Type I IFN therapy exists for other indications but constitutive low-dose approach has not been pursued clinically
refs: Zhou et al. 2016 PNAS doi:10.1073/pnas.1518240113

name: Big brown bat TNFα c-Rel motif → autoimmune therapy
description: c-Rel repressor binding motif in TNFα promoter restricts transcription under innate immune stimulation
outcome: No specific translation; anti-TNF biologics exist but are not bat-inspired
refs: Banerjee et al. 2017 Sci Rep doi:10.1038/s41598-017-01513-w

name: NMR hypoxia fructose switching → stroke therapy
description: Fructose-driven anaerobic glycolysis under anoxia; GluN2D NMDAR persists into adulthood
outcome: Conceptual interest; no drug program
refs: Park et al. 2017 Science doi:10.1126/science.aab3896
